question: How to make sure Engine Leap 1-A Fan cowl door fully locked: (CAT A, B1, B2)

options.text: Make sure 03 latch handles are fully locked and flush with the fan cowl surface and the flag on the LH Fan cowl door is fully retracted.
Make sure that all the latch handles stay in the fan cowl slots and are aligned with the adjacent fan cowl surface. And the key removed after turnback to lock position.

Use a SCREWDRIVER to push the pin towards the door locator then make sure that all the latches stay in the fan cowl slots and are aligned with the bottom of the adjacent cowl surface and the key removed.

sourceFile: 8. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How many Inector Nozzles are stage 1 High Pressure Turbine (HPT) Nozzle Assembly (CAT B1)

options.text: The stage 1 HPT nozzle assembly contains 18 two-nozzle segments for a total of 36 stator nozzle. Each stator nozzle has a thermal barrier coating and contains holes for cooling. The nozzle segment are supported at the inner band by the front inner HPT nozzle support.

The stage 1 HPT nozzle assembly contains 36 two-nozzle segments for a total of 36 stator nozzle. Each stator nozzle has a thermal barrier coating and contains holes for cooling. The nozzle segment are supported at the inner band by the front inner HPT nozzle support.

The stage 1 HPT nozzle assembly contains 24 two-nozzle segments for a total of 36 stator nozzle. Each stator nozzle has a thermal barrier coating and contains holes for cooling. The nozzle segment are supported at the inner band by the front inner HPT nozzle support.

sourceFile: 8. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Stage 2 HPT Nozzle Assembly operation? (CAT B1)

options.text: The HPT stator case and stage 1 HPT stator nozzle comprise the stationary portion of the HPT consisting of the stage 1 and stage 2 HPT stator hangers, the stage 1 and stage 2 HPT stator shrouds and stage 2 HPT stator nozzles. The HPT stator case is used to contain high-pressure high-temperature air.

The HPT stator case and stage 2 HPT stator nozzle comprise the stationary portion of the HPT consisting of the stage 1 and stage 2 HPT stator hangers, the stage 1 and stage 2 HPT stator shrouds and stage 2 HPT stator nozzles. The HPT stator case is used to contain high-pressure high-temperature air.

The HPT stator case and stage 2 HPT stator nozzle comprise the stationary portion of the HPT consisting of the stage 1 and stage 2 HPT stator hangers, the stage 1 and stage 2 HPT stator shrouds and stage 2 HPT stator nozzles. The HPT stator case is used to contain low-pressure low- temperature air.

question: Which statement is correct (Regarding to HP Fuel SOV)? (CAT B1)

options.text: If the selected master lever signal switches from ON to OFF when the engine is at idle and the aircraft is on the ground, two EEC channels energize the electro-hydraulic servo valve of the HP fuel SOV with EOS circuitry.

If the selected master lever signal transitions from ON to OFF position while the engine is not at idle or the aircraft is in the air, one of two EEC channels energizes the electro-hydraulic servo valve of the HP fuel SOV with EOS circuitry.

If the selected master lever signal transitions from ON to OFF position while the engine is not at idle or the aircraft is in the air, the two EEC channels energize their applicable EOS circuitry to ensure that fuel is promptly shut off.

The integrated STBY probe inside the sensor itself will be active.

question: Can Translating Cowl manual deploy when Deactived of the Thrust Reverser System for Ground Maintenance (the manual locking actuator is locked)? (CAT B1)

options.text: Yes.
Yes but the taks Deactivate the electrical tertiary lock must carry out.
No, Translating cowl can not deploy when Manual Locking actuator is locked.

ref: REF.: AMM 78-30-00-040-809-A Deactivation of the Thrust Reverser System for Ground

sourceFile: 8. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: After Thrust Reverser Cycling the T/R deploy more than 40 second the T/R can be stow as follow: (CAT B1)

options.text: Do Thrust Reverser (T/R) Cycling Test again.
Apply manual stow procedure.
Thrust reveser automatic stow after 50 second.

ref: REF.: AMM: 73-29-00-740-804-A: Thrust Reverser (T/R) Cycling test (menu mode)

sourceFile: 8. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How many type actuator of thrust reveser system? (CAT B1)
ref: REF.: AMM :78-31-00-00 CONF03 Thrust reverser Description and operation

sourceFile: 8. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How the left and right thrust reverser are synchronized? (CAT B1)

options.text: By Over the top flex shaft.
By the left and the right sync shaft.
The Two SFLA self adjusted.

ref: REF.: AMM : 78-31-00-00 CONF03 Thrust reverser Description and operation

sourceFile: 8. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Can Thrust reverser system work if Thrust reverser system Tertiary Lock inoperative? (CAT B1)

options.text: No.The thrust reverser must consider inoperative ref MEL:78-30-04A
Yes.The associated thrust reverser door tertiary lock is secured in the open position and the ENG REV LOCK with affected thrust reverser is not display on EWD after deactivation.
Yes. The associated thrust reverser door tertiary lock is secured in the close position and the ENG REV LOCK with affected thrust reverser is not display on EWD after deactivation.

ref: Ref MEL:78-30-04. Thrust Reverser Door Tertiary Lock

sourceFile: 8. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Do not OPEN the Thrust reverser doors if: (CAT A, B1, B2)

options.text: The wind speed is more than 40 Knots
The wind speed is more than 30 Knots
The wind speed is more than 50 Knots

ref: REF.: TASK 78-36-00-010-806-A Opening of the Thrust Reverser Cowl Doors

sourceFile: 8. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Do not connect the hold open rod before the thrust reverser cowl is open to: (CAT A, B1, B2)

options.text: 20 Degrees
40 Degrees
45 Degrees

ref: REF.: TASK 78-36-00-010-806-A Opening of the Thrust Reverser Cowl Doors

question: Where location oil tank of engines Leap1a (CAT A, B1, B2)

options.text: On the left-hand side of the Fan Case at the 4 o'clock aft looking forward position.
On the right-hand side of the Fan Case at the 4 o'clock aft looking forward position.
On the right-hand side of the Fan Case

Harness test (menu mode)
Idle test (menu mode)

sourceFile: 8. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How do you check for no fault of the Oil Debris Monitoring System of LEAP-1A : (CAT B1, B2)

options.text: FADEC test (menu mode)
Idle test (menu mode)
FADEC test (menu mode), verify that the message "ENGXB ODMS SENSOR MONITOR" is not displayed.

question: Where is the OPTS of LEAP-1A transmits EGN oil temperature and pressure to : (CAT B1, B2)

options.text: ECU
DMC
EEC

sourceFile: 8. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How to Oil Debris Reset and purpose chip count ? (CAT B1, B2)

options.text: On EEC channel A and count number of metallic chips that have been detected by the ODMS for the cumulative count during all flights
On EEC channel B and count number of metallic chips that have been detected by the ODMS for the cumulative count during all flights
Both EEC channel A+B and count number of metallic chips that have been detected by the ODMS for the cumulative count during all flights

sourceFile: 8. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: With oil pressure lower than 17.4 psi (1.20 bar) to 29 psi (2.00 bar) depeding N2 speed above ilde (CAT B1, B2)

options.text: Remove engine and return to shop if engines operative more 14s
Raised add interval 25 flight hours, 50 flight hours and 100 flight hours to check any presence of particles
With oil temperature more than 140 deg.C Remove engine and return to shop

ref: Ref AMM: 72-00-00-200-818-A - Inspection/Check After the Engine has Exceeded the

question: Which failsafe position of Starter Air Valve in the event of loss of solenoid control or transducer feedback signal? (CAT B1)

options.text: Open
Partially open
Close

sourceFile: 8. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What shoud you pay attention when manually Operate the Starter Air Valve in case of APU inop? (CAT B1)

options.text: Do not do a manual start override of the starter valve with the engine cross bleed or a ground start cart unless the engine is shut down for a minimum of 8 hours
Do not do a manual start override of the starter valve with the engine cross bleed or a ground start cart unless the engine is shut down for a minimum of 10 hours
Do not do a manual start override of the starter valve with the engine cross bleed or a ground start cart unless the engine is shut down for a minimum of 12 hours

sourceFile: 8. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: When is the Starter valve commanded closed in automatic start mode? (CAT B1)

options.text: N2 reached 45%
N2 reached 60%
N1 reached 55%

Replace the preformed packing on the pneumatic air starter shaft, the flange groove and the lubrication nozzle
Re torque the Nuts

sourceFile: 8. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B1 (100 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Which statement is correct? (CAT B1)

options.text: The Electronic Engine Controls (EECs) abort the automatic start, dry motor the engine and attempt an auto-restart for the following reasons: No light up (Exhaust Gas Temperature (EGT) low), Hung Start
The Electronic Engine Controls (EECs) abort the automatic start, dry motor the engine and attempt an auto-restart for the following reasons: No light up (Exhaust Gas Temperature (EGT) low), Hot start
The Electronic Engine Controls (EECs) abort the automatic start, dry motor the engine and attempt an auto-restart for the following reasons: No light up (Exhaust Gas Temperature (EGT) low), Stall
